evals.prompt: I have an idea for a service that monitors my portfolio positions across 3 exchanges (Binance, Bybit, Deribit), detects when any single position exceeds 5% of total NAV, and sends a Slack alert with the exact rebalancing trade needed to bring it back to target weight. I want you to turn this into a structured spec that an autonomous agent could execute to build this service.
evals.expected_output: A YAML+Markdown spec with clear inputs (exchange APIs, position data), outputs (Slack alert with trade details), constraints (real-time, multi-exchange auth), and a sequential workflow from data fetch to alert dispatch.

A YAML+Markdown spec that understands the existing code structure, defines inputs (the Backtester class, parameter grid, window config), outputs (walk-forward results with OOS metrics), constraints (must extend not rewrite the class), and a multi-step workflow for the rolling optimization loop.

evals.prompt:
Here's the relevant section from Binance's WebSocket API docs for order book streams:

- Endpoint: wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws/<symbol>@depth@100ms
- Payload: {"e":"depthUpdate","E":timestamp,"s":"BTCUSDT","U":first_update_id,"u":final_update_id,"b":[[price,qty],...],"a":[[price,qty],...]}
- To maintain a local order book: get a depth snapshot via REST, then apply each depthUpdate where U <= lastUpdateId+1 and u >= lastUpdateId+1
- Rate limit: 5 requests/sec for REST snapshots

I want to build a real-time order book aggregator that maintains synchronized L2 books for BTCUSDT across Binance and Bybit, computes cross-exchange spread and mid-price divergence, and logs snapshots to TimescaleDB every 100ms. Turn this into a spec an agent can build from.
evals.expected_output: A YAML+Markdown spec with inputs (WebSocket endpoints for both exchanges), outputs (TimescaleDB table with spread/divergence metrics), constraints (100ms latency target, proper sequence ID handling, reconnection logic), and a parallel workflow for the two exchange feeds merging into a single computation step.
